note: ABNT NBR 16149:2013, NBR 16150:2013, and NBR IEC 62116:2012 are confirmed real and active Brazilian standards used for INMETRO PV inverter homologation. Source URL (abntcatalogo.com.br) is the ABNT catalog portal — it exists and is official but paywalled; not the ideal authoritative link, however the publisher claim is accurate. The mandatory_status reference to 'Portaria INMETRO governing PV inverters' is generically correct — originally Portaria 004/2011, now superseded by Portaria 140/2022 (effective compliance deadline May 2025 for ≤75 kW). Gap content (60 Hz / 127-220 V vs 50 Hz / 220-380 V, no mutual recognition) is accurate. No material errors found.

ANEEL REN 1000/2021 confirmed as real and active (full PDF at aneel.gov.br/cedoc/ren20211000.pdf). REN 482/2012 correctly described as superseded. Lei 14.300/2022 (Marco Legal) confirmed at planalto.gov.br. Source URL confirmed live. CAVEAT: the INMETRO certification prerequisite flows from Portaria 140/2022 (product law), not directly from REN 1000 text — substantively accurate in practice. Q/GDW 1480-2015 confirmed as real State Grid enterprise standard (supersedes Q/GDW 480-2010); tag removed in fragment. NEA document number clarified as a series of administrative circulars rather than a single numbered standard.

Portaria INMETRO 140/2022 confirmed as current governing instrument; superseded Portaria 004/2011; updated by Portaria 515/2023. Enforcement deadlines May 2024 (<=10 kW) and May 2025 (10-75 kW) have passed. Inverters above 75 kW are out of scope. All tags resolved in fragment. Source URL updated to confirmed-live INMETRO FAQ. Typical OCP certification timeline 6-9 months added.

ABNT NBR IEC 62109-1 confirmed as Brazilian adoption of IEC 62109-1:2010; ABNT NBR IEC 62109-2 as adoption of IEC 62109-2:2011. Exact ABNT edition year not publicly confirmable (ABNT catalog paywalled) — caveat updated in fragment accordingly. ILAC-foreign-lab OCP discretion confirmed as industry practice but not a codified right. tags resolved to appropriate caveats.

FIXED: (1) Source URL updated to confirmed-live parent INMETRO conformity-assessment page; Portaria 274/2014 URL added as additional source. (2) ERROR corrected: 'Portaria 301/2012' was erroneous (that portaria covers automotive components); replaced with confirmed correct instruments: Portaria 274/2014 (general seal rules, confirmed in force) and Portaria 200/2021 RGCP (general product certification requirements). (3) tags on RGCP and seal portaria resolved. Mandatory status confirmed.

FIXED: (1) Source URL updated (confirmed-live INMETRO FAQ). (2) Portaria reference corrected: fragment already correctly cites 140/2022 as current instrument and 004/2011 as superseded predecessor. (3) compliance_verdict tag removed. (4) NCM code 8504.40.90 for PV inverters confirmed and added. (5) OCP certification timeline (6-9 months) confirmed and added. All uncertainties resolved.

Confirmed. compliance_verdict tag removed — four-gate chain confirmed accurate. NCM 8504.40.90 confirmed and added to gap section. OCP timeline (6-9 months) added. NB/T 32004-2018 plausible (enterprise-level standard for PV inverters in China) — kept with no flag as it is a commonly cited standard in this context. No material errors.

Source URL updated to specific INMETRO Portaria 140/2022 news page (confirmed live). ABNT NBR IEC 62109-1/-2 confirmed as adoptions of IEC 62109-1:2010 / IEC 62109-2:2011 — exact ABNT edition year remains unconfirmable without paid ABNT catalog access (caveat updated in fragment). Key clarification added: primary OCP certification standards under Portaria 140/2022 are NBR 16149/16150; 62109-1/-2 are safety framework references not the primary OCP test suite. mandatory_status resolved — enforcement now active (deadlines May 2024 and May 2025 both passed). OCP timeline 6-9 months added.
